item.title: 如何在 CML 上使用 Arista vEOS | How to use Arista vEOS on CML
item.description:
发表于在 CML (Cisco Modeling Labs) 中，除了标准平台之外，还可以自行定义和使用可用平台。

此过程显示在用户指南中。
在撰写本文时，任何注册用户都可以下载该软件，并介绍了其在各种网络模拟器中的使用示例。

设置 EVE-NG、CloudVision 门户和 vEOS
在 GNS3 1.5 中运行 vEOS
我们还可以看到尝试在 CML 中使用该系统的示例。

将 Arista 与 CML 结合使用
将 Arista 设备导入 Cisco CML
这次尝试在CML 2.2.3环境下（安装在之前搭建的ESXi主机上）设置EOS 4.28.1F，并能够开始使用。
以下是对此的记录。

我所做的细节

  • 从“工具”中选择“节点和图像定义”
  • 确认选择了“节点定义”选项卡，然后单击“添加”按钮。
  • 在“常规”字段中选择/输入以下信息
    • 编号：“veos”
    • 性质：“开关”
  • 在“用户界面”字段中选择/输入以下信息
    • 前缀：“veos-”
    • 图标：“开关”
    • 标签：“Arista vEOS”

  • 在“Linux Native Simulation”字段中选择/输入以下信息
    • 域驱动程序：“KVM”
    • 模拟驱动程序：“服务器”
    • 磁盘驱动程序：“IDE”
    • 内存：在这种情况下为“2048”
    • 网络驱动程序：“E1000”

  • 在“接口”字段中选择/输入以下信息

    • 环回名称：“Loopback0”
    • 串口数量：“1”
    • 默认物理接口数：本例中为“9”
    • 接口0：“管理1”

  • 在同一字段中，按“添加接口”按钮并输入以下信息

  • 在同一字段中，按“INCREMENT LAST INTERFACE”按钮，直到显示“Default number of physical interfaces”中指定的数字。

  • 在“启动”字段中输入以下信息

    • 超时：在这种情况下为“480”

  • 在同一字段中，单击“ADD BOOT LINE 按钮”并输入以下信息

  • 单击创建按钮

定义图像

  • 从“工具”中选择“节点和图像定义”
  • 选择“图像定义”选项卡
  • 点击“管理”按钮
  • 在“上传新图像文件”字段中选择“选择文件”并指定下载的 vEOS 文件
  • 点击“上传图片”按钮
  • 确认“Uploaded Images”字段中指定文件的显示后，按“CREATE NEW IMAGE DEFINITION”按钮。

  • 在“常规”字段中选择/输入以下信息

    • 标签：“Arista vEOS 4.28.1F”
    • 磁盘映像：上传的 vEOS 文件
    • 节点定义：定义的节点

  • 按“创建图像定义”按钮

操作确认

我使用注册的节点创建了一个实验室，并成功启动。 还确认了与相邻节点的通信是可能的。

补充信息及其他

在这项工作中没有使用 Aboot 文件。
此外，没有执行到 qcow2 的转换。

嵌入式系统 - 一旦暂停，rhira777 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，rhira777 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有 rhira777 的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 rhira777 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 rhira777 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 rhira777 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

rhira777

Posted on Aug 13

In CML (Cisco Modeling Labs), the available platforms can be defined and used by yourself in addition to the standard ones.

This procedure is shown in the user guide.
https://developer.cisco.com/docs/modeling-labs/#!custom-vm-images

Arista EOS also offers vEOS/cEOS available for virtual machines and containers.
At the time of this writing, the software is available for download by anyone with user registration, and examples of its use in various network simulators are introduced.

Setting up EVE-NG, CloudVision Portal and vEOS
https://aristanetworks.force.com/AristaCommunity/s/article/setting-up-eve-ng-cloudvision-portal-and-veos

vEOS/cEOS GNS3 Labs
https://aristanetworks.force.com/AristaCommunity/s/article/veos-ceos-gns3-labs

Running vEOS in GNS3 1.5
https://aristanetworks.force.com/AristaCommunity/s/article/running-veos-in-gns3-1-5

We can also see examples of attempts to use the system in CML.

Using Arista with CML
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Bg8aHL48n5g&t=2s

Importing Arista Devices into Cisco CML
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LfivGQa03sM&t=1166s

This time, I tried to set up EOS 4.28.1F in CML 2.2.3 environment (installed on the previously built ESXi host), and was able to start using it.
The following is a record of that.

Detail of what I did

  • Select "Node and Image Definition" from "TOOLS"
  • Confirm that the "Node Definition" tab is selected and click the "ADD" button.
  • Select/enter the following information in the "General" field
    • ID: "veos"
    • Nature: "switch"
  • Select/enter the following information in the "User Interface" field
    • Prefix: "veos-"
    • Icon: "switch"
    • Label: "Arista vEOS"

  • Select/enter the following information in the "Linux Native Simulation" field
    • Domain Driver: "KVM"
    • Simulation Driver: "server"
    • Disk Driver: "IDE"
    • Memory: "2048" in this case
    • CPUs: "2" in this case
    • CPU Limit: "100"
    • Network Driver: "E1000"

  • Select/enter the following information in the "Interfaces" field

    • Loopback name: "Loopback0"
    • Number of serial ports: "1"
    • Default number of physical interfaces: "9" in this case
    • interface 0："Management1"

  • In the same field, press the "ADD INTERFACE" button and enter the following information

  • In the same field, press the "INCREMENT LAST INTERFACE" button until the number specified in the "Default number of physical interfaces" is displayed.

  • Enter the following information in the "Boot" field

    • Timeout: "480" in this case

  • In the same field, click the "ADD BOOT LINE button" and enter the following information

  • Click the CREATE button

Define image

  • Select "Node and Image Definition" from "TOOLS"
  • Select the "Image Definition" tab
  • Click the "MANAGE" button
  • Select "Choose a file" in the "Upload New Image File" field and specify the downloaded vEOS file
  • Click the "UPLOAD IMAGE" button
  • After confirming the display of the file specified in the "Uploaded Images" field, press the "CREATE NEW IMAGE DEFINITION" button.

  • Select/enter the following information in the "General" field

    • ID: "veos-4-28-1f"
    • Label: "Arista vEOS 4.28.1F"
    • Disk Image: The uploaded vEOS file
    • Node Definition: The defined node

  • Press "CREATE IMAGE DEFINITION" button

Confirmation of operation

I created a lab using the registered node and it was successfully started. It was also confirmed that communication with neighboring nodes was possible.

Supplemental information and others

The Aboot file was not used in this work.
Also, the conversion to qcow2 was not performed.

Embedded Systems - Aug 13

Once suspended, rhira777 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, rhira777 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by rhira777 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If rhira777 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag rhira777:

rhira777 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging rhira777 will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: AWS 架构完善的框架：为什么您应该知道它与 AWS 一起使用 | AWS Well-Architected Framework: Why you should know it to work with AWS
item.description:
兰根·马赫什

发表于在云中构建应用程序和基础设施每天都变得越来越容易，了解对可扩展且高效的更好设计的需求非常重要。

拥有架构良好的系统可以极大地改善您的 AWS 环境的技术债务和业务成功的可能性。

卓越运营

  • 安全
  • 可靠性
  • 性能效率
  • 成本优化
  • 可持续性

    • 每个支柱的目的和重点

    卓越运营

    目的
    了解运营对于有效管理、监控和支持云工作负载的重要性。

    重点关注

    • 以代码形式执行操作
    • 进行频繁、小的、可逆的更改
    • 经常细化操作流程
    • 预测失败
    • 从所有操作失败中学习

    安全

    目的
    按照最佳实践保护您在 AWS 中的工作负载

    重点关注

    • 身份基础
    • 可追溯性
    • 检测
    • 数据保护
    • 事件响应

    了解有关安全支柱的更多信息

    可靠性

    目的
    可靠性支柱侧重于工作负载正确且一致地执行其预期功能的能力。 这包括在其整个生命周期内操作和测试工作负载的能力。

    重点关注

    • 自动从故障中恢复
    • 测试恢复程序
    • 水平扩展以消除单点故障
    • 自动更改
    • 停止猜测容量

    了解有关可靠性支柱的更多信息

    性能效率

    目的
    更好地利用云中的资源来管理需求。 了解如何随着需求变化和技术发展而提高效率。

    重点关注

    • 在 AWS 中有效使用更好的技术和工具
    • 使用 AWS 区域以更低的延迟提供更好的功能
    • 使用无服务器并消除服务器/应用程序的开销工作
    • 尝试找到最佳匹配
    • 使用符合您目标的技术

    成本优化

    目的
    构建和运营具有成本意识的工作负载，以实现业务成果，同时最大限度地降低成本并让您的组织最大限度地提高投资回报。

    重点关注

    • 实施云财务管理
    • 采用消费模式
    • 衡量整体效率

    可持续性

    目的
    了解所使用服务的长期环境、经济和社会影响。 量化和应用设计原则和最佳实践以减少这些影响

    重点关注

    • 了解您的影响
    • 建立可持续发展目标
    • 最大化利用率
    • 预测并采用新的、更高效的硬件和软件产品
    • 使用托管服务
    • 减少云工作负载的下游影响

    一旦暂停，ranganmahesh 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，ranganmahesh 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，ranganmahesh 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 ranganmahesh 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记ranganmahesh 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 ranganmahesh 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Rangan Mahesh

    Posted on Aug 13

    Building applications and infrastructure in the cloud is becoming easier everyday, it is very important to understand the need for better design that is scalable and efficient.

    The AWS Well-Architected Framework helps you understand the pros and cons of decisions you make while building systems on AWS. Having well-architected systems greatly improves technical debt of your AWS landscape and the likelihood of business success.

    The AWS Well-Architected framework focuses on the six pillars, namely:

    • Operational excellence
    • Security
    • Reliability
    • Performance efficiency
    • Cost optimization
    • Sustainability

    Purpose and Focus of each pillar

    Operational excellence

    Purpose
    Understand the importance of operations to effectively manage, monitor and support cloud workloads.

    Key Focus

    • Perform operations as code
    • Make frequent, small, reversible changes
    • Refine operations procedures frequently
    • Anticipate failure
    • Learn from all operational failures

    Learn more about Operational Excellence Pillar

    Security

    Purpose
    Secure your workload in AWS by following best practices

    Key Focus

    • Identity foundation
    • Traceability
    • Detection
    • Data protection
    • Incident response

    Learn more about Security Pillar

    Reliability

    Purpose
    The reliability pillar focuses on the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently. This includes the ability to operate and test the workload through its total lifecycle.

    Key Focus

    • Recover from failure automatically
    • Test recovery procedures
    • Scale horizontally to eliminate a single point of failure
    • Automate changes
    • Stop guessing capacity

    Learn more about Reliability Pillar

    Performance Efficiency

    Purpose
    Make better use of resources in the cloud to manage requirements. Understand how to handle efficiency as demand changes and technologies evolve.

    Key Focus

    • Effectively use better technology and tools in AWS
    • Use AWS Regions to provide better capabilities at lower latency
    • Go serverless and eliminate overhead work with servers/applications
    • Experiment to find your best match
    • Use technology that aligns with your goals

    Learn more about Performance Efficiency Pillar

    Cost Optimization

    Purpose
    Build and operate cost-aware workloads that achieve business outcomes while minimizing costs and allowing your organization to maximize its return on investment.

    Key Focus

    • Implement cloud financial management
    • Adopt a consumption model
    • Measure overall efficiency

    Learn more about Cost Optimization Pillar

    Sustainability

    Purpose
    Understand the long-term environmental, economic, and societal impact of the services used. Quantify and apply design principles and best practices to reduce these impacts

    Key Focus

    • Understand your impact
    • Establish sustainability goals
    • Maximize utilization
    • Anticipate and adopt new, more efficient hardware and software offerings
    • Use managed services
    • Reduce the downstream impact of your cloud workloads

    Learn more about Cost Optimization Pillar

    Once unsuspended, ranganmahesh will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by ranganmahesh will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If ranganmahesh is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging ranganmahesh will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 如何在 CML 上使用 SONiC | How to use SONiC on CML
item.description:
发表于在 CML (Cisco Modeling Labs) 中，除了标准平台之外，还可以自行定义和使用可用平台。

此过程显示在用户指南中。
适用于 Windows 的虚拟机上的 SONiC
如何使用虚拟交换机 (VM)
这一次，我尝试在 CML 2.2.3 环境下（安装在之前搭建的 ESXi 主机上）为虚拟机设置使用 SONiC 镜像，并且能够开始使用它。
以下是对此的记录。

我所做的细节

  • 在这种情况下，从以下获取
    • 下载虚拟交换机镜像文件
      • 这次从Branch 202205中选择一个
      • 从“工具”中选择“节点和图像定义”
      • 确认选择了“节点定义”选项卡，然后单击“添加”按钮。
      • 在“常规”字段中选择/输入以下信息
        • 性质：“开关”
      • 在“用户界面”字段中选择/输入以下信息
        • 前缀：“sonicvsvm-”
        • 图标：“开关”
        • 标签：“SONiC 虚拟交换机 (VM)”

      • 在“Linux Native Simulation”字段中选择/输入以下信息

        • 域驱动程序：“KVM”
        • 模拟驱动程序：“服务器”
        • 磁盘驱动程序：“VirtIO”
        • 内存：在这种情况下为“2048”
        • 网络驱动程序：“E1000” 在“接口”字段中选择/输入以下信息

          • 环回名称：“Loopback0”
          • 串口数量：“1”
          • 默认物理接口数：本例中为“9”
          • 接口0：“eth0”

        • 在同一字段中，按“添加接口”按钮，直到显示“默认物理接口数量”中指定的数量，然后输入以下信息

          • 接口 1：“以太网 0”
          • 接口 2：“以太网 4”
          • 接口 3：“以太网 8”
          • 接口 4：“以太网 12”
          • 接口 5：“以太网 16”
          • 接口 6：“以太网 20”
          • 接口 7：“以太网 24”
          • 接口 8：“以太网 28”

        • 在“启动”字段中输入以下信息

          • 超时：在这种情况下为“480”

        • 在同一字段中，单击“ADD BOOT LINE 按钮”并输入以下信息

        • 单击创建按钮

定义图像

  • 从“工具”中选择“节点和图像定义”
  • 选择“图像定义”选项卡
  • 点击“管理”按钮
  • 在“上传新图像文件”字段中选择“选择文件”并指定下载的 SONiC 图像
  • 点击“上传图片”按钮
  • 确认“Uploaded Images”字段中指定文件的显示后，按“CREATE NEW IMAGE DEFINITION”按钮。

  • 在“常规”字段中选择/输入以下信息

    • 标签：“SONiC 虚拟交换机 (VM) 202205”
    • 磁盘镜像：上传的SONiC镜像
    • 节点定义：定义的节点

  • 按“创建图像定义”按钮

操作确认

我使用注册的节点创建了一个实验室，并成功启动。 还确认了与相邻节点的通信是可能的。

补充信息及其他

默认用户名/密码是 admin/YourPaSsWoRd。

启动日志

 Booting `SONiC-OS-202205.133812-e8fe21b3a' Loading SONiC-OS OS kernel ... 一旦暂停，rhira777 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，rhira777 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有 rhira777 的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 rhira777 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 rhira777 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 rhira777 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

rhira777

Posted on Aug 13

In CML (Cisco Modeling Labs), the available platforms can be defined and used by yourself in addition to the standard ones.

This procedure is shown in the user guide.
https://developer.cisco.com/docs/modeling-labs/#!custom-vm-images

SONiC is also available for virtual machines and containers.

SONiC on virtual machine for Windows
https://github.com/sonic-net/SONiC/wiki/SONiC-on-virtual-machine-for-Windows

HOWTO Use Virtual Switch (VM)
https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-buildimage/blob/master/platform/vs/README.vsvm.md

HOWTO Use Virtual Switch (Docker)
https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-buildimage/blob/master/platform/vs/README.vsdocker.md

This time, I tried to set up using SONiC image for virtual machines in CML 2.2.3 environment (installed on the previously built ESXi host), and was able to start using it.
The following is a record of that.

Detail of what I did

  • In this case, obtain from the following
    • https://sonic.software/
    • Download the virtual switch image file
      • Select the one from Branch 202205 this time
      • "sonic-vs.img.gz".
  • Select "Node and Image Definition" from "TOOLS"
  • Confirm that the "Node Definition" tab is selected and click the "ADD" button.
  • Select/enter the following information in the "General" field
    • ID: "sonicvsvm"
    • Nature: "switch"
  • Select/enter the following information in the "User Interface" field
    • Prefix: "sonicvsvm-"
    • Icon: "switch"
    • Label: "SONiC Virtual Switch (VM)"

  • Select/enter the following information in the "Linux Native Simulation" field

    • Domain Driver: "KVM"
    • Simulation Driver: "server"
    • Disk Driver: "VirtIO"
    • Memory: "2048" in this case
    • CPUs: "2" in this case
    • CPU Limit: "100"
    • Network Driver: "E1000" ---

  • Select/enter the following information in the "Interfaces" field

    • Loopback name: "Loopback0"
    • Number of serial ports: "1"
    • Default number of physical interfaces: "9" in this case
    • interface 0："eth0"

  • In the same field, press the "ADD INTERFACE" button until the number specified in the "Default number of physical interfaces" is displayed, and enter the following information

    • interface 1: "Ethernet0"
    • interface 2: "Ethernet4"
    • interface 3: "Ethernet8"
    • interface 4: "Ethernet12"
    • interface 5: "Ethernet16"
    • interface 6: "Ethernet20"
    • interface 7: "Ethernet24"
    • interface 8: "Ethernet28"

  • Enter the following information in the "Boot" field

    • Timeout: "480" in this case

  • In the same field, click the "ADD BOOT LINE button" and enter the following information

  • Click the CREATE button

Define image

  • Select "Node and Image Definition" from "TOOLS"
  • Select the "Image Definition" tab
  • Click the "MANAGE" button
  • Select "Choose a file" in the "Upload New Image File" field and specify the downloaded SONiC image
  • Click the "UPLOAD IMAGE" button
  • After confirming the display of the file specified in the "Uploaded Images" field, press the "CREATE NEW IMAGE DEFINITION" button.

  • Select/enter the following information in the "General" field

    • ID: "sonicvsvm-202205"
    • Label: "SONiC Virtual Switch (VM) 202205"
    • Disk Image: The uploaded SONiC image
    • Node Definition: The defined node

  • Press "CREATE IMAGE DEFINITION" button

Confirmation of operation

I created a lab using the registered node and it was successfully started. It was also confirmed that communication with neighboring nodes was possible.

Supplemental information and others

The default username/password is admin/YourPaSsWoRd.

startup log 

  Booting `SONiC-OS-202205.133812-e8fe21b3a'


Loading SONiC-OS OS kernel ...

Loading SONiC-OS OS initial ramdisk ...

item.title: ezy - 桌面 gRPC 客户端 | ezy - desktop gRPC client
item.description:
阿列克谢·瓦休科夫

发表于你好！ 我刚刚发布了名为ezy的新桌面 gRPC 客户端。

为什么还有一个？
我每天都在使用 gRPC，在我看来，所有现有的 GUI 客户端都没有精心设计或没有完全支持 gRPC 功能。 不幸的是，其中一些像BloomRPC没有维护。

易

特征

计划

此处提供实际路线图。

如果您对此项目感兴趣，请在此处或在讨论中分享您的反馈/想法🙏

一旦暂停，notmedia 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，notmedia 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，notmedia 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 notmedia 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 notmedia 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 notmedia 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Alexey Vasyukov

Posted on Aug 13

Hi! I just released new desktop gRPC client called ezy.

Why another one?
I'm working with gRPC every day and for my opinion all existed GUI clients do not well designed or do not have fully gRPC features support. And some of them like BloomRPC unfortunately are unmaintained.

ezy

Features

✅ Localy persisted collections. ✅ Multi-tabs.

✅ Environments.

gRPC

✅ Unary calls. ✅ Client/Server/Bidirectional streaming. ✅ Metadata support. ✅ TLS (Server-side/Mutual) support.

✅ Good errors output.

Plans

Actual roadmap available here.

If you are interested in this project, please share your feedback/ideas here or in discussions 🙏
